title: Active Management and Performance Measurement: An investor's risk aversion coefficient of A=4 suggests that the...
summary: Interpret the risk aversion coefficient in the investor utility function.
prompt: An investor's risk aversion coefficient of A=4 suggests that the investor is relatively risk-seeking.
solution: A higher risk aversion coefficient (A>1) indicates that the investor is risk-averse, preferring safer investments to the higher potential returns of riskier assets.
